children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: a Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such funds as Congress may determine necessary to support research on specific national or regional animal health or disease problems, or national or regional problems relating to pre-harvest, on-farm food safety, or animal well-being, but not to exceed $35,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 1991 through 2012, and not in excess of such sums as may after , be authorized by law for any subsequent fiscal year.September 29, 1977

children.heading: Duration of grants
children.label: b Duration of grants
children.text: Notwithstanding the provisions of , funds appropriated under this section shall be awarded in the form of grants, for periods not to exceed five years, to State agricultural experiment stations, colleges and universities (including 1890 Institutions (as defined in )), other research institutions and organizations, Federal agencies, private organizations or corporations, and individuals.section 3197 of this titlesection 7601 of this title

children.heading: Establishment of annual priority lists for allocation of funds
children.label: c Establishment of annual priority lists for allocation of funds

children.text: any health or disease problem which causes or may cause significant economic losses to any part of the livestock production industry;
any food safety problem that has a significant pre-harvest (on-farm) component and is recognized as posing a significant health hazard to the consuming public;
issues of animal well-being related to production methods that will improve the housing and management of animals to improve the well-being of livestock production species;
whether current scientific knowledge necessary to prevent, cure, or abate such a health or disease problem is adequate; and
whether the status of scientific research is such that accomplishments may be anticipated through the application of scientific effort to such health or disease problem.

children.heading: Assignment of priorities for grants
children.label: d Assignment of priorities for grants
children.text: Without regard to any consultation under subsection (c), the Secretary shall, to the extent feasible, award grants on the basis of the priorities assigned through a peer review system. Grantees shall be selected on a competitive basis in accordance with such procedures as the Secretary may establish.

children.heading: Distribution of multiyear grants
children.label: e Distribution of multiyear grants
children.text: In the case of multiyear grants, the Secretary shall distribute funds to grant recipients on a schedule which is reasonably related to the timetable required for the orderly conduct of the research project involved.

children.heading: Applicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: f Applicability of chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5 and title XVIII of this Act [ et seq.] shall not apply to a panel or board created solely for the purpose of reviewing applications or proposals submitted under this subchapter.7 U.S.C. 2281
